req.title: Account-level transaction limits
req.statement: A customer shall configure daily per-channel limits within regulatory caps; changes take effect after a 2-hour cool-down.

doc.reasoning: R-FIN-017 requires testing account-level transaction limit configuration with regulatory enforcement, cool-down state management, and error handling. The test suite applies EP (limit types/channels), BVA (regulatory cap boundaries), STC (pending→effective state), and NEG (422 over-cap) heuristics to ensure deterministic, traceable coverage of the /accounts/{id}/limits endpoint.

tests.name: test_set_limit_within_cap_returns_202_pending
preconditions: User is authenticated with valid auth token
Account {account_id} exists and belongs to authenticated user
No pending limit change exists for this account
expected: effective_at is approximately 2 hours from now (±5 min tolerance)
trace: R-FIN-017: Expected outcomes: pending limit stored; effective after cool-down
tests.executable:
def test_set_limit_within_cap_returns_202_pending(auth_token, account_id):
    url = f'http://localhost:8000/accounts/{account_id}/limits'
    headers = {'Authorization': f'Bearer {auth_token}'}
    payload = {'daily_limit': 5000, 'channel': 'online'}
    resp = <requests_post>(url, json=payload, headers=headers)
    assert resp.status_code == 202
    body = resp.json()
    assert body['status'] == 'pending'
    assert 'effective_at' in body
    effective = <parse_iso_timestamp>(body['effective_at'])
    now = <utc_now>()
    delta = (effective - now).total_seconds()
    assert 7100 < delta < 7300, f'Cool-down {delta}s not ~2h'

tests.name: test_set_limit_over_regulatory_cap_returns_422
preconditions: User is authenticated with valid auth token
Account {account_id} exists and belongs to authenticated user
Regulatory cap for channel 'online' is known to be 50000
actions: Assert response status code and error details
expected: No pending limit is created in database
trace: R-FIN-017: Error pathways: 422 when over regulatory cap
R-FIN-017: changes take effect after a 2-hour cool-down (implies validation before storage)
tests.executable:
def test_set_limit_over_regulatory_cap_returns_422(auth_token, account_id):
    url = f'http://localhost:8000/accounts/{account_id}/limits'
    headers = {'Authorization': f'Bearer {auth_token}'}
    payload = {'daily_limit': 75000, 'channel': 'online'}
    resp = <requests_post>(url, json=payload, headers=headers)
    assert resp.status_code == 422
    body = resp.json()
    assert body['error'] == 'LIMIT_EXCEEDS_CAP'
    assert body['cap'] == 50000
    pending = <query_pending_limits>(account_id)
    assert len(pending) == 0

tests.name: test_set_limit_at_exact_regulatory_cap_boundary
preconditions: User is authenticated with valid auth token
Account {account_id} exists and belongs to authenticated user
Regulatory cap for channel 'atm' is exactly 10000
actions: Assert response indicates acceptance at boundary
expected: Pending limit is stored with daily_limit = 10000
trace: R-FIN-017: A customer shall configure daily per-channel limits within regulatory caps
BVA: Test at exact boundary (cap value)

tests.name: test_limit_becomes_effective_after_cooldown_transition
preconditions: User is authenticated with valid auth token
Account {account_id} exists and belongs to authenticated user
A pending limit with effective_at = now + 2 hours exists in database
actions: Advance system time by 2 hours 1 minute (or query after that interval)
expected: Limit value remains consistent across state transition
trace: R-FIN-017: changes take effect after a 2-hour cool-down
R-FIN-017: Expected outcomes: pending limit stored; effective after cool-down
tests.executable:
def test_limit_becomes_effective_after_cooldown_transition(auth_token, account_id):
    url = f'http://localhost:8000/accounts/{account_id}/limits'
    headers = {'Authorization': f'Bearer {auth_token}'}
    resp1 = <requests_get>(url, headers=headers)
    assert resp1.json()['status'] == 'pending'
    initial_limit = resp1.json()['daily_limit']
    <wait_or_mock_time_advance>(121 * 60)
    resp2 = <requests_get>(url, headers=headers)
    assert resp2.json()['status'] == 'effective'
    assert resp2.json()['daily_limit'] == initial_limit

tests.name: test_set_limit_multiple_channels_isolation
preconditions: User is authenticated with valid auth token
Account {account_id} exists and belongs to authenticated user
expected: GET returns array with both online and mobile entries
Each entry maintains independent status and effective_at
trace: EP: Different channels are distinct equivalence classes
tests.executable:
def test_set_limit_multiple_channels_isolation(auth_token, account_id):
    url = f'http://localhost:8000/accounts/{account_id}/limits'
    headers = {'Authorization': f'Bearer {auth_token}'}
    resp1 = <requests_post>(url, json={'daily_limit': 5000, 'channel': 'online'}, headers=headers)
    resp2 = <requests_post>(url, json={'daily_limit': 3000, 'channel': 'mobile'}, headers=headers)
    assert resp1.status_code == 202
    assert resp2.status_code == 202
    resp_get = <requests_get>(url, headers=headers)
    limits = resp_get.json()['limits']
    assert len(limits) >= 2
    channels = {l['channel'] for l in limits}
    assert 'online' in channels and 'mobile' in channels
